_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how cohesion in texts is improved by strengthening internal structures — using evidence, quotations and references to connect and support ideas.

_source.w: We are learning how cohesion in texts is improved by strengthening internal structures — using evidence, quotations and references to connect and support ideas.
y: Strong cohesion with evidence and references elevates writing from opinion to informed analysis.
These skills are essential for academic writing, research and professional communication.
Understanding how evidence creates cohesion helps us evaluate the strength of others' arguments.
eg: We analyse how a well-written essay uses evidence and quotations to support and link its arguments.
We practise incorporating evidence smoothly into our own writing using signal phrases.
We evaluate the cohesion of a text by examining how well its internal structures connect ideas.
We discuss how different types of evidence — quotes, statistics, examples — serve different cohesive functions.
We improve the cohesion of a draft text by strengthening its evidence and internal links.
We compare texts with strong and weak cohesion and identify what makes the difference.
